Container.Name: marquee
Container.Overview:
Self-hosted media metadata dashboard - TMDb + Plex + Sonarr/Radarr. Look up any actor, studio, or franchise, see instantly whether it's already in your Plex library or being downloaded, and send anything missing straight to Sonarr or Radarr.
REQUIRES A POSTGRES DATABASE. This template runs only the Marquee app itself - install a Postgres container first (the official "postgres" Community Applications template works fine), then set DATABASE_URL below to point at it, e.g. postgres://marquee:YOURPASSWORD@POSTGRES_CONTAINER_IP:5432/marquee
First visit after install creates the one-time admin account. Full setup instructions: https://github.com/TimmyAmant/marquee#readme

Config.Name: DATABASE_URL
Config.Description: Postgres connection string. Install a Postgres container first (e.g. the official 'postgres' CA template), then point this at it: postgres://USER:PASSWORD@CONTAINER_IP:5432/DBNAME

Config.Name: AUTH_SECRET
Config.Description: Any long random string. Generate with: openssl rand -base64 32

Config.Name: MASTER_ENCRYPTION_KEY
Config.Description: Base64 32-byte key used to encrypt saved Sonarr/Radarr/Plex credentials. Generate with: openssl rand -base64 32 -- BACK THIS UP, losing it makes saved credentials undecryptable.

Config.Name: AUTH_TRUST_HOST
Config.Description: Leave as true for LAN access without a fixed domain

Config.Name: TMDB_API_KEY
Config.Description: From themoviedb.org/settings/api. Can also be set later from within the app: Settings -> Integrations.

Config.Name: TMDB_ACCESS_TOKEN
Config.Description: Alternative to TMDB_API_KEY (TMDb v4 access token) - only need one of the two

Config.Name: TVDB_API_KEY
Config.Description: From thetvdb.com/api-information - used to cross-reference Sonarr's shows to TMDb
